methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Slip Robotics is a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $44M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company operating in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector at similar or proximate stages. The private peer set includes 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, with valuations ranging from $350M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a baseline proxy of $44M and applying a typical Series B forward step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $84M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics subsectors and company maturity within the Series B cohort.

The triangulated valuation range is $71M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $579M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects material uncertainty about whether Slip Robotics should be valued toward the lower or upper end of its peer distribution. Key sensitivities include the company's specific robotics application, gross margin profile relative to peers, and path to differentiated unit economics. Additional diligence on revenue run-rate and customer concentration would help narrow this range.
